title: The Greatest Pulpit, Preacher and Sermon
summary: “And lo a voice from heaven, saying, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased.”(Matthew 3:17). “While he yet spake, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them: and behold a voice out of the cloud, which said, This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him.”(Matthew 17:5) All God-fearing and God-seeking people had made their way to the river Jordan that day. A spirit of repentance and humility had seized them, and they knew that they had to go and hear the words of righteous John and be baptized in preparation for the coming of the Messiah. Only when people prepare to repent and make their way to the preacher (church) to make things right with God, will they hear a voice from heaven. What an amazing thing happened that day – humans heard a voice from heaven! This was the greatest pulpit in the history of preaching and the Father himself was the preacher. And this was the greatest sermon in the history of preaching – “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased”. Once again, on the Mount of Transfiguration, Peter spoke but was cut off by the voice from heaven. Why would God cut off the words of his leading Apostle? What message was greater than that of the man with the keys of heaven? What did it say? It said: “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him”. May God always cut us off when we stray away from this message! I would be very happy for this voice to cut me off right now in the middle of sermon and declare to everyone hearing, “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased; hear ye him”.
